attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite
2	
3	Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
4	(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.
5	
6	## Progress
7	
8	| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
9	|---|---|---|
10	| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
11	| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
12	| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | done |
13	| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
14	| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
15	| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
16	| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | done |
17	| I1 | `demo.sh` runner + `make demo` target | done |
18	| I2 | CTest `switching-mode` label registration | done |
19	| I3 | `run_e2e.sh` integration | done |
20	
21	One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
22	completion (done).
23	
24	## Status Legend
25	
26	- not-started: demo has not begun
27	- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
28	- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered
29	
30	## Dependency DAG
31	
32	```
33	T1 ---+
34	T2 ---+
35	T3 ---+
36	T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
37	T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
38	T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
39	```
40	
41	T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.
42	
43	## Demos
44	
45	### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)
46	
47	- Status: done
48	- Type: behavioral
49	- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
50	  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
51	  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
52	- Key files:
53	  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
54	- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
55	- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".
56	
57	### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)
58	
59	- Status: done
60	- Type: behavioral
61	- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
62	  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
63	- Key files:
64	  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
65	- Dependencies: none.
66	- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".
67	
68	### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)
69	
70	- Status: done
71	- Type: behavioral
72	- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
73	  -> vsim `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain. Both PCs verified inside the
74	  RVC loop body (0x100002 / 0x100004). QEMU2 handback is intentionally
75	  skipped: icount_spin has no natural semihosting exit, and using
76	  `--qemu-binary` would hang. M8 is covered by T1 / T2 / T6.
77	- Key files:
78	  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`
79	- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
80	- Pass criterion: both QEMU1 and vsim exit 200 with state files whose PC
81	  lies inside the icount_spin loop.
82	
83	### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)
84	
85	- Status: done
86	- Type: behavioral
87	- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
88	  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
89	  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
90	- Key files:
91	  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
92	- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
93	- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
94	  slice indices and weight sum (informational).
95	
96	### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 + M3)
97	
98	- Status: done
99	- Type: behavioral
100	- Description: Demonstrates trigger-mode mixing on a CSR-less workload.
101	  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
102	  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
103	  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
104	  semihosting exit.
105	- Key files:
106	  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
107	- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
108	  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
109	- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
110	  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.
111	
112	### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)
113	
114	- Status: done
115	- Type: behavioral
116	- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
117	  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
118	  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
119	  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
120	  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
121	- Key files:
122	  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
123	- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
124	- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
125	  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.
126	
127	### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)
128	
129	- Status: done
130	- Type: behavioral
131	- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline on rt_c_v_regs.
132	  (1) Profile: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=20,icount=2000 -> 15 BBV rows.
133	  (2) Cluster: simpoint -maxK 2 -dim 2 -> 2 cluster representatives.
134	  (3) Per phase: QEMU+plugin slice=20,slice_at=K -> drain; archive via
135	      `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive` Python helper into
136	      $WORKDIR/snap/K/.
137	  (4) Per archive: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim`
138	      hydrates the mmap, vsim drains naturally on csrwi 0x7C0,1.
139	  Defaults baked for rt_c_v_regs (SLICE=20, ICOUNT=2000); other fixtures
140	  may need different env overrides.
141	- Key files:
142	  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
143	- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
144	  Implementation note: skipped a per-phase YAML template; the
145	  orchestrator's drain argv builder does not yet thread slice/slice_at,
146	  so per-phase QEMU drains are direct invocations and archive writes
147	  use the Python helper directly. Orchestrator IS used for replay.
148	- Pass criterion: K phase archives created with valid state.bin + mem +
149	  meta.json; K replays exit 0; final tally line printed.
150	
151	## Final Integration
152	
153	### I1 - demo.sh runner + make demo target
154	
155	- Status: done
156	- Type: behavioral
157	- Description: User-facing entry point. `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
158	  walks T1..T7 sequentially, prints per-mode banners, and emits a
159	  pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<regex>` to subset. Makefile gets a
160	  `demo` phony target that runs `all` then `demo.sh`.
161	- Key files:
162	  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
163	  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` (add `demo` target)
164	
165	### I2 - CTest switching-mode label registration
166	
167	- Status: done
168	- Description: `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach added to
169	  `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (mirrors the `VSIM_RT_FIXTURES`
170	  pattern at lines 128-170). Seven CTest entries with labels
171	  `hybrid-e2e;switching-mode` and per-demo COST (T1/T2/T4 = 30s,
172	  T3/T5/T6 = 60s, T7 = 180s). `ctest -L switching-mode` runs the
173	  suite; takes effect on the next `build_vsim.sh` reconfigure.
174	- Key files:
175	  - `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
176	
177	### I3 - run_e2e.sh integration
178	
179	- Status: done
180	- Description: Added a `DEMO_TESTS` array next to `NEG_TESTS` in
181	  `run_e2e.sh`, with one entry per demo. Demos run once each (no
182	  csr/pc loop because each script hard-codes its trigger). Workplan
183	  appends `demo|name|cmd` tuples; summary line gained a `demo` column.
184	  `icount_spin` added to `REQUIRED_ELFS` (used by T3/T4/T5/T7).
185	  Smoke-tested: `FILTER=demo_csr_oneshot bash run_e2e.sh` runs T1 and
186	  reports `demo 1p/0f/0s`.
187	- Key files:
188	  - `run_e2e.sh`
189

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | done |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | done |
| I1 | `demo.sh` runner + `make demo` target | done |
| I2 | CTest `switching-mode` label registration | done |
| I3 | `run_e2e.sh` integration | done |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> vsim `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain. Both PCs verified inside the
  RVC loop body (0x100002 / 0x100004). QEMU2 handback is intentionally
  skipped: icount_spin has no natural semihosting exit, and using
  `--qemu-binary` would hang. M8 is covered by T1 / T2 / T6.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: both QEMU1 and vsim exit 200 with state files whose PC
  lies inside the icount_spin loop.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
  slice indices and weight sum (informational).

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 + M3)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Demonstrates trigger-mode mixing on a CSR-less workload.
  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
  semihosting exit.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline on rt_c_v_regs.
  (1) Profile: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=20,icount=2000 -> 15 BBV rows.
  (2) Cluster: simpoint -maxK 2 -dim 2 -> 2 cluster representatives.
  (3) Per phase: QEMU+plugin slice=20,slice_at=K -> drain; archive via
      `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive` Python helper into
      $WORKDIR/snap/K/.
  (4) Per archive: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim`
      hydrates the mmap, vsim drains naturally on csrwi 0x7C0,1.
  Defaults baked for rt_c_v_regs (SLICE=20, ICOUNT=2000); other fixtures
  may need different env overrides.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
  Implementation note: skipped a per-phase YAML template; the
  orchestrator's drain argv builder does not yet thread slice/slice_at,
  so per-phase QEMU drains are direct invocations and archive writes
  use the Python helper directly. Orchestrator IS used for replay.
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created with valid state.bin + mem +
  meta.json; K replays exit 0; final tally line printed.

## Final Integration

### I1 - demo.sh runner + make demo target

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: User-facing entry point. `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
  walks T1..T7 sequentially, prints per-mode banners, and emits a
  pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<regex>` to subset. Makefile gets a
  `demo` phony target that runs `all` then `demo.sh`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` (add `demo` target)

### I2 - CTest switching-mode label registration

- Status: done
- Description: `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach added to
  `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (mirrors the `VSIM_RT_FIXTURES`
  pattern at lines 128-170). Seven CTest entries with labels
  `hybrid-e2e;switching-mode` and per-demo COST (T1/T2/T4 = 30s,
  T3/T5/T6 = 60s, T7 = 180s). `ctest -L switching-mode` runs the
  suite; takes effect on the next `build_vsim.sh` reconfigure.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`

### I3 - run_e2e.sh integration

- Status: done
- Description: Added a `DEMO_TESTS` array next to `NEG_TESTS` in
  `run_e2e.sh`, with one entry per demo. Demos run once each (no
  csr/pc loop because each script hard-codes its trigger). Workplan
  appends `demo|name|cmd` tuples; summary line gained a `demo` column.
  `icount_spin` added to `REQUIRED_ELFS` (used by T3/T4/T5/T7).
  Smoke-tested: `FILTER=demo_csr_oneshot bash run_e2e.sh` runs T1 and
  reports `demo 1p/0f/0s`.
- Key files:
  - `run_e2e.sh`

1	# Comprehensive E2E Test Plan: Switching-Mode Demonstration
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	The hybrid simulator now ships **all eight switching mechanisms** (F1-F12) that select when QEMU yields to vsim and vice versa, but the test surface treats them as isolated plugin checks. There is no single artifact a new user (or contributor) can run that demonstrates every switching mode end-to-end, and the SimPoint-driven flow - the highest-leverage mode for production benchmarking - has no integrated test that chains BBV emit -> SimPoint clustering -> phase replay.
6	
7	This plan adds:
8	1. One **demonstration e2e fixture per switching mode**, each named after the mode it shows off, runnable via a single `make demo` target.
9	2. The **SimPoint integrated loop** (currently the only F-feature without a chain-of-modes test): BBV profile -> simpoint binary -> archive each chosen phase -> replay each phase under vsim.
10	3. A **mode-matrix CTest section** in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` so every switching mode has a labeled CTest entry and shows up under `ctest -L switching-mode`.
11	
12	Intended outcome: a user can run `make demo` and watch QEMU<->vsim swap drivers eight different ways in under five minutes; a contributor breaking any switching mode gets a labeled CTest failure that names the mode.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Switching Mode Catalog (eight modes, today)
17	
18	| # | Mode | Trigger source | Today's test |
19	|---|---|---|---|
20	| M1 | **CSR insn match** | `csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1}` in binary | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=csr) |
21	| M2 | **PC symbol resolve** | `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` ELF symbols | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=pc) |
22	| M3 | **Icount** (F5/F6) | plugin `icount=N`, vsim `--hybrid-icount N` | `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` (QEMU only) |
23	| M4 | **BBV-emit** (F7) | plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N` | `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` |
24	| M5 | **Slice-consume** (F11) | plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` | `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` (QEMU only) |
25	| M6 | **Archive emit** (F9) | orchestrator YAML `on_exit.archive_as` | unit test only |
26	| M7 | **Checkpoint replay** (F10) | `orchestrator replay-checkpoint <dir>` | unit test only |
27	| M8 | **End-of-program** | QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit | implicit in every Tier-2 e2e |
28	
29	Coverage gaps (the targets of this plan):
30	- **M3** has no vsim-side e2e (vsim receives plugin-drained state and resumes).
31	- **M5** has no orchestrator-level e2e (drain at slice K -> resume under vsim -> finish).
32	- **M6+M7** have no e2e at all - both are unit-tested in isolation; never wired through a real round-trip.
33	- **No fixture chains M4 -> simpoint binary -> M5 -> M6 -> M7** (the SimPoint integrated loop).
34	
35	---
36	
37	## New Tests to Add (one per mode)
38	
39	All seven new e2e drivers live under `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/` and reuse the existing `icount_spin.elf` workload (CSR-less spin at 0x100000), `rt_c_v_regs.elf` for state-rich modes, plus the orchestrator CLI. Each driver writes its own short log preamble naming the mode it exercises.
40	
41	### T1. `demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (covers M1, M8)
42	Re-thin wrapper over `roundtrip_e2e.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Justification: a single self-documenting script that prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1)" before invoking the harness. No new code, just a labeled entrypoint for the demo runner.
43	
44	### T2. `demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (covers M2)
45	Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path runs end-to-end.
46	
47	### T3. `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M3 end-to-end)
48	- Drives `icount_spin.elf` through QEMU plugin with `icount=10000`.
49	- Plugin drains at insn 10000 -> exit 200.
50	- Orchestrator hands state to vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` (vsim drains 5k cycles later).
51	- vsim exits 200; spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub; QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit.
52	- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0, drained PC in `icount_spin` body both times.
53	- Why new: today only `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` exists, and it terminates after QEMU1 drain. The vsim resume path under icount mode is untested at e2e level.
54	
55	### T4. `demo_bbv_profile.sh` (covers M4 + tool integration)
56	- Generalize the existing `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` so it leaves the `.bb` file in `$LOG_DIR/bbv/icount_spin.bb` instead of `mktemp`.
57	- Run `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile bbv/icount_spin.bb -maxK 8 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights weights`.
58	- Assert the output files exist, parse them, print the chosen slice indices + weights as the demo output.
59	- Pass criterion: simpoint binary exits 0, weights sum to 1.0 (+/-1e-3).
60	- No vsim involved; this is the QEMU-only profiling pass.
61	
62	### T5. `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M5 end-to-end + M8)
63	- Read the `simpts` file from T4's output (or accept `SLICE_AT=K` env override).
64	- Drive `icount_spin.elf` with `slice=1000,slice_at=K,icount=<large fallback>`.
65	- Plugin drains exactly at insn (K+1) * 1000.
66	- vsim resumes, runs ROI, drains via CSR or icount (configurable), QEMU2 finishes.
67	- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body, QEMU2 exit 0.
68	- Why new: today `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` is QEMU-only.
69	
70	### T6. `demo_archive_replay.sh` (NEW; covers M6 + M7 end-to-end)
71	- Step 1: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root $LOG_DIR/snap --workdir $LOG_DIR/run1`
72	  - Asserts `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist.
73	  - Validates `meta.json` schema=1, abi_magic matches, mem_size matches mmap.
74	- Step 2: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint $LOG_DIR/snap/0 --resume-with vsim --workdir $LOG_DIR/run2`
75	  - Asserts replay session exits 0.
76	  - Asserts post-replay state.bin differs from `snap/0/state.bin` (PC advanced).
77	- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; archive shape + replay validation match.
78	- Why new: F9/F10 exist only as unit tests today; no test wires both through an actual mmap + state file.
79	
80	### T7. `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (NEW; the centerpiece, covers M4 -> simpoint -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)
81	The full SimPoint-driven workflow, the production use case for hybrid sim:
82	
83	```
84	1. Profile pass (M4)
85	   $ qemu+plugin icount_spin.elf bbv_out=bbv.bb slice=1000 icount=10000
86	   -> 10-row bbv.bb
87	
88	2. Cluster (tools/simpoint)
89	   $ simpoint -loadFVFile bbv.bb -maxK 4 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights w
90	   -> simpts: list of (slice_idx, cluster_id); w: cluster weights
91	
92	3. For each chosen slice_idx in simpts: (M5 + M6)
93	   $ orchestrator run plan_phase_${idx}.yaml --archive-root snap --workdir run_${idx}
94	     YAML:
95	       step 1: qemu with slice=1000,slice_at=${idx} -> drain
96	       on_exit: archive_as: snap/${idx}
97	   -> snap/${idx}/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
98	
99	4. For each archive: (M7)
100	   $ orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/${idx} --resume-with vsim --workdir replay_${idx}
101	   -> vsim resumes from cluster-representative state, runs ROI, optional QEMU2 finish
102	```
103	
104	Driver script generates the per-phase YAMLs from a template, invokes simpoint, walks both loops, and asserts:
105	- Each phase archive validates.
106	- Each replay exits 0 (or a documented non-zero with a clear reason for end-of-program phases).
107	- Final wall-clock printout: `phases_replayed=K, total_vsim_cycles=N, baseline_cycles=M, speedup=M/N`.
108	
109	Pass criterion: K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. The speedup line is informational, not asserted (deterministic only on `icount_spin`).
110	
111	### Demo runner: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
112	A single user-facing entry point. Sequentially invokes T1..T7, printing a banner per mode and a final pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<mode-name>` env to skip subsets. Runs in under five minutes on the CI host when the build is cached.
113	
114	```sh
115	make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo      # alias for ./demo.sh
116	```
117	
118	---
119	
120	## Mode-Matrix CTest Section
121	
122	Add to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` after the existing plugin tests:
123	
124	```cmake
125	# Switching-mode demo set. Each test asserts one of the eight modes
126	# (M1..M8) end-to-end. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
127	# the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`.
128	set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
129	  "demo_csr_oneshot:M1+M8:CSR insn match -> end-of-program"
130	  "demo_pc_oneshot:M2:PC symbol resolve"
131	  "demo_icount_roundtrip:M3:Icount trigger E2E"
132	  "demo_bbv_profile:M4:BBV emit + SimPoint clustering"
133	  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:M5:Slice-consume drain E2E"
134	  "demo_archive_replay:M6+M7:Checkpoint archive + replay"
135	  "demo_simpoint_loop:M4->M5->M6->M7:Full SimPoint pipeline"
136	)
137	foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
138	  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
139	  list(GET parts 0 fname)
140	  list(GET parts 1 mode)
141	  list(GET parts 2 desc)
142	  add_test(NAME test_${fname}
143	    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${fname}.sh)
144	  set_tests_properties(test_${fname} PROPERTIES
145	    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
146	    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
147	    COST 60)
148	endforeach()
149	```
150	
151	Each test honors `SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77` so missing simpoint binary / missing plugin / missing ELFs skip cleanly rather than failing the suite on a fresh checkout.
152	
153	---
154	
155	## Critical Files
156	
157	To create (no existing implementation):
158	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
159	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
160	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`
161	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
162	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
163	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
164	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
165	- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh` - user-facing runner
166	- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` - parameterized SimPoint phase YAML used by T7
167	
168	To modify:
169	- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach (see above)
170	- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - new `demo` phony target invoking `demo.sh`
171	- `run_e2e.sh` - extend `RT_TESTS` with the seven demo entries so the host runner picks them up (single `MODE=demo` knob optional)
172	
173	To reuse without modification:
174	- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - already supports every plugin-side switching arg
175	- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint` - F12 clustering binary
176	- `tools/orchestrator/main.py` + `tools/orchestrator/examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` - YAML driver and example
177	- `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py` (`write_archive`, `replay_checkpoint`, `validate_replay_meta`) - F9+F10 entry points
178	- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_plugin_{bbv_emit,phase_consume,icount_trigger}.sh` - reference implementations for plugin invocation patterns
179	- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/{icount_spin.S,rt_c_v_regs.c}` - workloads
180	- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{handoff_controller,resume_driver,state_drain,qemu_handback}.hpp` - the entire vsim-side switching mechanism
181	
182	---
183	
184	## Verification
185	
186	End-to-end ladder, each step requires the previous:
187	
188	1. **Build**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` builds all referenced ELFs (already exist; rule is just an alias for `make demo.sh`).
189	2. **Single-mode**: `bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh` exits 0 and prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1) - PASS".
190	3. **Plugin-only modes**: `bash demo_bbv_profile.sh` produces a `.bb` file, runs simpoint, prints chosen slices + weights.
191	4. **Vsim-resume modes**: `bash demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` and `bash demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` each exit 0 with a "round-trip complete" banner.
192	5. **Orchestrator modes**: `bash demo_archive_replay.sh` creates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` and replays exit 0.
193	6. **Centerpiece**: `bash demo_simpoint_loop.sh` runs the full M4->M5->M6->M7 chain; K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. Speedup line printed.
194	7. **All modes**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` runs T1..T7 sequentially, prints a final tally `7/7 PASS` in under 5 minutes.
195	8. **CTest integration**: `ctest -L switching-mode --output-on-failure` from the host build dir runs the same set under CTest's harness; each mode shows up as a labeled test.
196	9. **Skip behavior**: on a fresh container without `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`, T4 and T7 skip (return 77); CTest reports `Skipped` not `Failed`.
197	10. **Regression detection**: deliberately break the BBV emitter (e.g., off-by-one in slice boundary) and verify T4 fails with `weights do not sum to 1.0` and T7 fails with `simpts file empty`. Deliberately break F10 (e.g., skip `validate_replay_meta`) and verify T6 fails with `archive validation rejected`.
198	
199	---
200	
201	## Out of Scope
202	
203	- **Phase 4 Kanata-loop integration** (M9-equivalent): no test added; depends on `--kanata-loop-start/end` orchestrator plumbing that doesn't exist yet. Documented as the next mode to add when Phase 4 lands.
204	- **Multi-hart switching**: `_NDS_NHART <= 2` is supported by the wire struct but the resume/drain path is only exercised on hart 0; per `HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` L6.
205	- **Performance assertion in T7**: the speedup line is informational. A fully-RTL baseline run for the same icount window is a separate work item (Phase 4 verification).
206	- **State-coverage expansion** (FP/M-CSR/PMP round-trip): orthogonal to switching modes; tracked elsewhere as Phase 5 hardening.
207	- **CTest parallel scheduling for the demo set**: each demo runs sequentially under the runner; `ctest -j` can still schedule them concurrently but the `make demo` path stays linear so the banner output is readable.
208

file.content:
# Comprehensive E2E Test Plan: Switching-Mode Demonstration

## Context

The hybrid simulator now ships **all eight switching mechanisms** (F1-F12) that select when QEMU yields to vsim and vice versa, but the test surface treats them as isolated plugin checks. There is no single artifact a new user (or contributor) can run that demonstrates every switching mode end-to-end, and the SimPoint-driven flow - the highest-leverage mode for production benchmarking - has no integrated test that chains BBV emit -> SimPoint clustering -> phase replay.

This plan adds:
1. One **demonstration e2e fixture per switching mode**, each named after the mode it shows off, runnable via a single `make demo` target.
2. The **SimPoint integrated loop** (currently the only F-feature without a chain-of-modes test): BBV profile -> simpoint binary -> archive each chosen phase -> replay each phase under vsim.
3. A **mode-matrix CTest section** in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` so every switching mode has a labeled CTest entry and shows up under `ctest -L switching-mode`.

Intended outcome: a user can run `make demo` and watch QEMU<->vsim swap drivers eight different ways in under five minutes; a contributor breaking any switching mode gets a labeled CTest failure that names the mode.

---

## Switching Mode Catalog (eight modes, today)

| # | Mode | Trigger source | Today's test |
|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | **CSR insn match** | `csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1}` in binary | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=csr) |
| M2 | **PC symbol resolve** | `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` ELF symbols | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=pc) |
| M3 | **Icount** (F5/F6) | plugin `icount=N`, vsim `--hybrid-icount N` | `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M4 | **BBV-emit** (F7) | plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N` | `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` |
| M5 | **Slice-consume** (F11) | plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` | `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M6 | **Archive emit** (F9) | orchestrator YAML `on_exit.archive_as` | unit test only |
| M7 | **Checkpoint replay** (F10) | `orchestrator replay-checkpoint <dir>` | unit test only |
| M8 | **End-of-program** | QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit | implicit in every Tier-2 e2e |

Coverage gaps (the targets of this plan):
- **M3** has no vsim-side e2e (vsim receives plugin-drained state and resumes).
- **M5** has no orchestrator-level e2e (drain at slice K -> resume under vsim -> finish).
- **M6+M7** have no e2e at all - both are unit-tested in isolation; never wired through a real round-trip.
- **No fixture chains M4 -> simpoint binary -> M5 -> M6 -> M7** (the SimPoint integrated loop).

---

## New Tests to Add (one per mode)

All seven new e2e drivers live under `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/` and reuse the existing `icount_spin.elf` workload (CSR-less spin at 0x100000), `rt_c_v_regs.elf` for state-rich modes, plus the orchestrator CLI. Each driver writes its own short log preamble naming the mode it exercises.

### T1. `demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (covers M1, M8)
Re-thin wrapper over `roundtrip_e2e.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Justification: a single self-documenting script that prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1)" before invoking the harness. No new code, just a labeled entrypoint for the demo runner.

### T2. `demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (covers M2)
Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path runs end-to-end.

### T3. `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M3 end-to-end)
- Drives `icount_spin.elf` through QEMU plugin with `icount=10000`.
- Plugin drains at insn 10000 -> exit 200.
- Orchestrator hands state to vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` (vsim drains 5k cycles later).
- vsim exits 200; spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub; QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit.
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0, drained PC in `icount_spin` body both times.
- Why new: today only `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` exists, and it terminates after QEMU1 drain. The vsim resume path under icount mode is untested at e2e level.

### T4. `demo_bbv_profile.sh` (covers M4 + tool integration)
- Generalize the existing `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` so it leaves the `.bb` file in `$LOG_DIR/bbv/icount_spin.bb` instead of `mktemp`.
- Run `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile bbv/icount_spin.bb -maxK 8 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights weights`.
- Assert the output files exist, parse them, print the chosen slice indices + weights as the demo output.
- Pass criterion: simpoint binary exits 0, weights sum to 1.0 (+/-1e-3).
- No vsim involved; this is the QEMU-only profiling pass.

### T5. `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M5 end-to-end + M8)
- Read the `simpts` file from T4's output (or accept `SLICE_AT=K` env override).
- Drive `icount_spin.elf` with `slice=1000,slice_at=K,icount=<large fallback>`.
- Plugin drains exactly at insn (K+1) * 1000.
- vsim resumes, runs ROI, drains via CSR or icount (configurable), QEMU2 finishes.
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body, QEMU2 exit 0.
- Why new: today `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` is QEMU-only.

### T6. `demo_archive_replay.sh` (NEW; covers M6 + M7 end-to-end)
- Step 1: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root $LOG_DIR/snap --workdir $LOG_DIR/run1`
  - Asserts `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist.
  - Validates `meta.json` schema=1, abi_magic matches, mem_size matches mmap.
- Step 2: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint $LOG_DIR/snap/0 --resume-with vsim --workdir $LOG_DIR/run2`
  - Asserts replay session exits 0.
  - Asserts post-replay state.bin differs from `snap/0/state.bin` (PC advanced).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; archive shape + replay validation match.
- Why new: F9/F10 exist only as unit tests today; no test wires both through an actual mmap + state file.

### T7. `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (NEW; the centerpiece, covers M4 -> simpoint -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)
The full SimPoint-driven workflow, the production use case for hybrid sim:

```
1. Profile pass (M4)
   $ qemu+plugin icount_spin.elf bbv_out=bbv.bb slice=1000 icount=10000
   -> 10-row bbv.bb

2. Cluster (tools/simpoint)
   $ simpoint -loadFVFile bbv.bb -maxK 4 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights w
   -> simpts: list of (slice_idx, cluster_id); w: cluster weights

3. For each chosen slice_idx in simpts: (M5 + M6)
   $ orchestrator run plan_phase_${idx}.yaml --archive-root snap --workdir run_${idx}
     YAML:
       step 1: qemu with slice=1000,slice_at=${idx} -> drain
       on_exit: archive_as: snap/${idx}
   -> snap/${idx}/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

4. For each archive: (M7)
   $ orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/${idx} --resume-with vsim --workdir replay_${idx}
   -> vsim resumes from cluster-representative state, runs ROI, optional QEMU2 finish
```

Driver script generates the per-phase YAMLs from a template, invokes simpoint, walks both loops, and asserts:
- Each phase archive validates.
- Each replay exits 0 (or a documented non-zero with a clear reason for end-of-program phases).
- Final wall-clock printout: `phases_replayed=K, total_vsim_cycles=N, baseline_cycles=M, speedup=M/N`.

Pass criterion: K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. The speedup line is informational, not asserted (deterministic only on `icount_spin`).

### Demo runner: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
A single user-facing entry point. Sequentially invokes T1..T7, printing a banner per mode and a final pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<mode-name>` env to skip subsets. Runs in under five minutes on the CI host when the build is cached.

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo      # alias for ./demo.sh
```

---

## Mode-Matrix CTest Section

Add to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` after the existing plugin tests:

```cmake
# Switching-mode demo set. Each test asserts one of the eight modes
# (M1..M8) end-to-end. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
# the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`.
set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
  "demo_csr_oneshot:M1+M8:CSR insn match -> end-of-program"
  "demo_pc_oneshot:M2:PC symbol resolve"
  "demo_icount_roundtrip:M3:Icount trigger E2E"
  "demo_bbv_profile:M4:BBV emit + SimPoint clustering"
  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:M5:Slice-consume drain E2E"
  "demo_archive_replay:M6+M7:Checkpoint archive + replay"
  "demo_simpoint_loop:M4->M5->M6->M7:Full SimPoint pipeline"
)
foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
  list(GET parts 0 fname)
  list(GET parts 1 mode)
  list(GET parts 2 desc)
  add_test(NAME test_${fname}
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${fname}.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_${fname} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
    COST 60)
endforeach()
```

Each test honors `SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77` so missing simpoint binary / missing plugin / missing ELFs skip cleanly rather than failing the suite on a fresh checkout.

---

## Critical Files

To create (no existing implementation):
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh` - user-facing runner
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` - parameterized SimPoint phase YAML used by T7

To modify:
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach (see above)
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - new `demo` phony target invoking `demo.sh`
- `run_e2e.sh` - extend `RT_TESTS` with the seven demo entries so the host runner picks them up (single `MODE=demo` knob optional)

To reuse without modification:
- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - already supports every plugin-side switching arg
- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint` - F12 clustering binary
- `tools/orchestrator/main.py` + `tools/orchestrator/examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` - YAML driver and example
- `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py` (`write_archive`, `replay_checkpoint`, `validate_replay_meta`) - F9+F10 entry points
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_plugin_{bbv_emit,phase_consume,icount_trigger}.sh` - reference implementations for plugin invocation patterns
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/{icount_spin.S,rt_c_v_regs.c}` - workloads
- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{handoff_controller,resume_driver,state_drain,qemu_handback}.hpp` - the entire vsim-side switching mechanism

---

## Verification

End-to-end ladder, each step requires the previous:

1. **Build**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` builds all referenced ELFs (already exist; rule is just an alias for `make demo.sh`).
2. **Single-mode**: `bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh` exits 0 and prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1) - PASS".
3. **Plugin-only modes**: `bash demo_bbv_profile.sh` produces a `.bb` file, runs simpoint, prints chosen slices + weights.
4. **Vsim-resume modes**: `bash demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` and `bash demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` each exit 0 with a "round-trip complete" banner.
5. **Orchestrator modes**: `bash demo_archive_replay.sh` creates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` and replays exit 0.
6. **Centerpiece**: `bash demo_simpoint_loop.sh` runs the full M4->M5->M6->M7 chain; K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. Speedup line printed.
7. **All modes**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` runs T1..T7 sequentially, prints a final tally `7/7 PASS` in under 5 minutes.
8. **CTest integration**: `ctest -L switching-mode --output-on-failure` from the host build dir runs the same set under CTest's harness; each mode shows up as a labeled test.
9. **Skip behavior**: on a fresh container without `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`, T4 and T7 skip (return 77); CTest reports `Skipped` not `Failed`.
10. **Regression detection**: deliberately break the BBV emitter (e.g., off-by-one in slice boundary) and verify T4 fails with `weights do not sum to 1.0` and T7 fails with `simpts file empty`. Deliberately break F10 (e.g., skip `validate_replay_meta`) and verify T6 fails with `archive validation rejected`.

---

## Out of Scope

- **Phase 4 Kanata-loop integration** (M9-equivalent): no test added; depends on `--kanata-loop-start/end` orchestrator plumbing that doesn't exist yet. Documented as the next mode to add when Phase 4 lands.
- **Multi-hart switching**: `_NDS_NHART <= 2` is supported by the wire struct but the resume/drain path is only exercised on hart 0; per `HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` L6.
- **Performance assertion in T7**: the speedup line is informational. A fully-RTL baseline run for the same icount window is a separate work item (Phase 4 verification).
- **State-coverage expansion** (FP/M-CSR/PMP round-trip): orthogonal to switching modes; tracked elsewhere as Phase 5 hardening.
- **CTest parallel scheduling for the demo set**: each demo runs sequentially under the runner; `ctest -j` can still schedule them concurrently but the `make demo` path stays linear so the banner output is readable.

content.text: TEST_ROADMAP.md fully done. Let me check for related uncommitted plan files and the recent docs.

content.content:
1	# Hybrid Simulator: QEMU + Verilator (vsim)
2	
3	A plan to extend `vsim` from a pure Verilator cycle-accurate simulator into a hybrid functional/RTL simulator that runs the boring code in QEMU at MHz speed and switches into Verilator only for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), then optionally hands back.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Context
8	
9	`vsim` runs at 5-9 KHz on Andes RISC-V cores. A typical workload spends >99% of cycles in boot, libc init, and warm-up before reaching the kernel under study. Today the only escape hatch is `--save`/`--continue`, which still requires an initial RTL run to reach the snapshot point. There is also already a `--kanata-loop-{start,end,skip,record}` mechanism (`src/main.cpp:183-214`) that identifies the interesting loop iterations.
10	
11	We want: run everything before the ROI in QEMU (functional, MHz), hand the architectural state to Verilator at the ROI boundary, run the ROI cycle-accurately to record kanata/clarity pipeline data, and optionally hand back to QEMU to finish.
12	
13	The intended outcome: 100x-1000x reduction in wall-clock time for benchmarking workflows that only care about steady-state pipeline behavior of a small kernel, with byte-identical kanata output for the recorded window vs. a fully-RTL baseline.
14	
15	---
16	
17	## 2. Tutorial: Why This Is Technically Feasible
18	
19	### 2.1 The state-exchange problem, reframed
20	
21	A naive hybrid sim has to "transfer" CPU state between two simulators. In practice the state splits into three categories with very different costs:
22	
23	| Category | Items | Size | Strategy |
24	|---|---|---|---|
25	| Architectural regs | x0..x31, f0..f31, V regs, key CSRs | 4-8 KB | **Copy** via well-defined ABI struct |
26	| Memory | guest RAM, ILM/DLM | MB-GB | **Share** via `mmap` - zero copy |
27	| Microarchitectural state | TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer | KB-MB | **Discard** - let cold starts repopulate |
28	
29	The key trick: **memory does not move**. QEMU runs with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/vsim-ram`. vsim's `SimpleMemory::data` (`src/platform/simple_memory.hpp:20`) is changed to an `mmap` of the same fd. Both simulators read and write the same physical bytes. The same trick is used by QEMU's CPR (`docs/devel/migration/CPR.rst`) and gem5's `SimpleSwitchableProcessor`. RAM size becomes irrelevant.
30	
31	Microarch state is intentionally discarded. We accept cold caches/BPU on entry into Verilator and rely on the existing `kanata_loop_skip N` mechanism to warm them up before recording. This matches the gem5 "switch + warmup" pattern.
32	
33	### 2.2 The trigger: a magic CSR convention
34	
35	We need both sides to agree on "the ROI starts here." Reuse the user-defined CSR space at `0x7C0`:
36	
37	```asm
38	csrwi 0x7C0, 0   # enter detail (QEMU -> vsim)
39	csrwi 0x7C0, 1   # exit detail  (vsim -> QEMU)
40	```
41	
42	- **QEMU** sees this via a TCG plugin: `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()` with a per-instruction filter that pattern-matches the encoding of `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` (mask `0xfff03073` against the 32-bit insn). On hit, the plugin drains state and exits.
43	- **vsim** sees this via the existing retire signal taps in `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp`. We pre-scan the ELF for PCs containing `csrw 0x7C0, *` (LIEF is already linked - `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp`), then watch for retire-of-PC against that set.
44	
45	Phase 4 lifts this further: `--kanata-loop-start <pc>` and `--kanata-loop-end <pc>` already specify the ROI by PC. The orchestrator wraps these as PC-trigger events and **the binary needs no magic CSR at all**.
46	
47	We deliberately avoid `tohost`/`fromhost` (HTIF reserved) and `ecall` (already used by the runtime).
48	
49	### 2.3 The write-back path: existing JTAG/DTM, in-process
50	
51	This is the highest-leverage finding. vsim already has:
52	
53	- `src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp` - a full JTAG DTM implementation with the RISC-V Debug Module
54	- `--jtag-port` CLI flag and OpenOCD support (`src/main.cpp:148-154`)
55	
56	The RISC-V Debug Spec gives Abstract Commands that read/write GPRs (`regno` 0x1000-0x101F), FPRs (0x1020-0x103F), and CSRs (0x0000-0x0FFF) directly via the Debug Module. **Writing all 32 GPRs + 32 FPRs + ~20 critical CSRs is ~85 DMI transactions**, which is ~hundreds of cycles - negligible compared to the millions of cycles we save.
57	
58	We drive the DTM **in-process**, calling its `handle_dmi_*` methods directly without an OpenOCD socket. No DPI shim, no Verilog modifications, no per-CPU-variant code (works for all ten CPU wrappers in `src/cpu_wrappers/`).
59	
60	The sequence to "resume from QEMU state":
61	
62	1. Reset CPU; halt request via DM
63	2. For each register in the ABI struct, issue Abstract Command (`regno` + data)
64	3. Set DPC = saved PC
65	4. Issue `dret` via Program Buffer
66	
67	Vector registers and any CPU-specific custom CSR that DM doesn't expose are deferred to Phase 5. If DM coverage is incomplete we add a per-CPU Program Buffer fallback or, last resort, an mmap-shared V regfile via DPI.
68	
69	### 2.4 The QEMU side: a TCG plugin against the existing build
70	
71	`/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64` is QEMU **9.2.0** (verified). This is recent enough for the full plugin API:
72	
73	- `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()` - per-insn callback
74	- `qemu_plugin_get_registers()` / `qemu_plugin_read_register()` - drain GPR/FP/CSR
75	- `qemu_plugin_request_exit()` - stop QEMU cleanly
76	
77	Templates already on disk: `/local/nick/qemu_v5/contrib/plugins/{execlog.c, hotblocks.c, bbv.c}`. We add one new plugin `hybrid_handoff.c` next to them. No QEMU patches, no fork.
78	
79	### 2.5 What does NOT work and is out of scope
80	
81	- **MMIO inside the ROI**: device state lives in QEMU C and has no mirror in RTL. Document; fail loudly via an interconnect watch in Phase 5.
82	- **lr/sc reservations across handoff**: invalidate at handoff; do not transfer.
83	- **Self-modifying code in the ROI**: the magic CSR is a synchronization boundary by convention.
84	- **mcycle/minstret continuity**: monotonic across handoffs but not equal to a fully-RTL run; documented discontinuity.
85	- **Andes-internal QEMU fork** (`andescycle-dev-v0.16-17-g03dfe23.tar.gz`): treated as opaque. Plan against mainline QEMU 9.2.
86	
87	---
88	
89	## 3. Architecture at a Glance
90	
91	```
92	                              shared mmap RAM (/dev/shm/vsim-ram)
93	                                           |
94	              +----------------------------+----------------------------+
95	              |                                                         |
96	              v                                                         v
97	       QEMU 9.2.0 + plugin                                    vsim (SystemC + Verilator)
98	       +---------------------+                                +-----------------------+
99	       | TCG insn loop       |     state.bin (ABI struct)     | SystemC main loop     |
100	       | hybrid_handoff.so   |  <-->  [ABI v1 packed]   <-->  | jtag_dtm in-process   |
101	       | csrw 0x7C0 -> drain |                                | csr_trigger -> drain  |
102	       +---------------------+                                +-----------------------+
103	              ^                                                         ^
104	              |                                                         |
105	              +--orchestrator script (vsim-hybrid)----------------------+
106	                  enter-pc / exit-pc mode also supported
107	```
108	
109	State exchange contract: a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (header + GPR + FPR + CSR + V) defined in `hybrid/include/state_abi.h`, written atomically (`O_TMPFILE` + `linkat`) at handoff.
110	
111	---
112	
113	## 4. Design Decisions (locked)
114	
115	| # | Decision | Rationale |
116	|---|---|---|
117	| a | **JTAG/DTM in-process** for register write-back (vs. DPI shim or synthetic insn stream) | Existing infrastructure (`src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`); zero RTL changes; works across all 10 CPU wrappers |
118	| b | Same for CSRs; Program Buffer fallback for unreachable ones | Same |
119	| c | **Modify `SimpleMemory` in-place**: backing becomes `std::span<uint8_t>` with optional mmap; existing `std::vector` path retained when mmap unset | All call sites use `.data()`/`.size()` only - both span and vector satisfy. Templated class used as concrete member; forking is worse |
120	| d | **TCG plugin** (in-process, fastest) against existing QEMU 9.2.0 build at `/local/nick/qemu_v5` | Plugin API is feature-complete in 9.x; templates available; no fork |
121	| e | **Packed C struct** in shared file (vs. protobuf/JSON) | C-and-C++-compatible single header; zero parse cost; static_assert offsets to catch drift |
122	
123	---
124	
125	## 5. Phased Implementation Plan
126	
127	Each phase lands a working subset and is independently testable.
128	
129	### Phase 0 - Plumbing, validation, ABI freeze
130	
131	**Goal**: skeleton in place, riskiest assumptions de-risked, no runtime behavior change.
132	
133	Deliverables:
134	- `hybrid/include/state_abi.h` - versioned packed struct: magic `'AHYB'`, version, flags, pc, priv, gpr[32], fpr[32], M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, mcycle, minstret, satp, pmpcfg[16], pmpaddr[64]), V state (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vreg[32 * MAX_VLEN_BYTES]), 256B trailing reserved.
135	- `hybrid/include/state_abi_check.h` - `static_assert` on offsets and size.
136	- `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - adds `VSIM_QEMU_ROOT` cache var (default `/local/nick/qemu_v5`), build option `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`.
137	- `hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl` - 50-line OpenOCD script connecting to vsim's DM, probing which `regno` ranges return success. **De-risks the JTAG decision.** Document output in `hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md`.
138	
139	Test: vsim builds with `-DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`, plugin builds against `/local/nick/qemu_v5`, struct `static_assert`s pass.
140	
141	Risk to escalate: if DM Abstract Commands cannot reach vector regs / Andes-custom CSRs, Phase 5 must add Program Buffer or DPI - decide before starting Phase 2.
142	
143	### Phase 1 - Shared mmap RAM
144	
145	**Goal**: vsim runs identically to today, but main memory is mmap-backed when `--shared-mem-path` is set.
146	
147	Files modified:
148	- `src/platform/simple_memory.hpp:20` - `std::vector<uint8_t> data` becomes a `std::span<uint8_t> data` plus an optional owning `std::vector<uint8_t> owned_storage_` plus a `bool owns_mapping_` flag. Add a constructor taking `(int fd, size_t offset, size_t size)` that mmaps in.
149	- `src/platform/platform.hpp` - thread mmap fd from `Simulator` into `main_memory` construction via a `std::optional<MmapBacking>` member.
150	- `src/main.cpp` - new flags `--shared-mem-path <file>`, `--shared-mem-size <bytes>`. When unset, behave as today.
151	
152	Files NOT modified (already span-compatible): `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp`, `src/simulator_context.hpp` (uses `.data()`/`.size()` only), `src/simulator.hpp:190,228-230`.
153	
154	Test: `tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.cpp` - subprocess launches `vsim --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/test_ram --shared-mem-size 256M small_loop.elf`, parent process mmaps the same file, verifies `.text` bytes match objdump and that an in-program store is visible to the parent.
155	
156	### Phase 2 - One-way handoff: QEMU -> vsim
157	
158	**Goal**: program runs under QEMU, hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`, QEMU drains state and exits. vsim is invoked with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>` and runs from the resumed PC to program end.
159	
160	New files:
161	- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - modeled on `/local/nick/qemu_v5/contrib/plugins/execlog.c`. TB callback walks insns; per-insn callback registered only on `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` matches. On fire, dispatch on `rs1` value (0=enter, 1=exit), drain GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, write packed struct atomically, call `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
162	- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/meson.build` - builds `libhybrid_handoff.so`.
163	- `src/hybrid/qemu_resume.hpp` - reads ABI struct, validates magic/version, drives DM via `jtag_dtm` directly (no socket): halt -> for each reg issue Abstract Command -> set DPC -> `dret`.
164	
165	Files modified:
166	- `src/main.cpp` - new flag `--resume-from-qemu <file>`, mutually exclusive with `--continue`.
167	- `src/simulator.hpp:239` - `reset()` extended with "resume from external state" branch that runs after `resetn` is asserted; reuses the existing JTAG infrastructure in-process.
168	
169	Test: tiny RV64 program:
170	```asm
171	_start:
172	  csrwi 0x7C0, 0
173	  li a0, 1; li a1, 2; add a2, a0, a1
174	  li a7, 93; ecall
175	```
176	Compile, run under QEMU+plugin, observe state file. Run vsim with `--resume-from-qemu state.bin`, verify `a2 == 3` at retire and exit code 0.
177	
178	### Phase 3 - Round-trip: vsim -> QEMU
179	
180	**Goal**: vsim detects exit-detail magic CSR write, drains state, hands back to QEMU on the same shared RAM.
181	
182	New files:
183	- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - `CsrTrigger` class. ELF pre-scan computes the set of PCs containing `csrw 0x7C0, *`. Subscribes to retire signals (`wb_i0_pc`/`wb_i0_retire` from `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp`). On retire-of-PC in the set, decode the saved insn to get `rs1` register, read GPR via existing probe (`gpr_index_hart0`/`gpr_value_hart0` in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:91-92`). If value=1, signal handoff.
184	- `src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - dual of `qemu_resume.hpp`: halt CPU, read all archregs via DM Abstract Commands, populate ABI struct, msync to file.
185	
186	Files modified:
187	- `src/simulator.hpp:289-340` (the `run()` loop) - add `csr_trigger.tick(cpu)` after the per-cycle checks. On drain signal, break loop with new exit code `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
188	- `src/main.cpp` - on `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU` plus `--qemu-binary` set, after `sim.cpu.final()` (line 342), `execve` QEMU with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=<shared-mem-path>` and `-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,resume_from=<state.bin>`. The plugin's `resume_from` mode pokes state back via `qemu_plugin_write_register()` at the first TB callback.
189	
190	Test: extend Phase 2 program with `csrwi 0x7C0, 1; ecall` after the body. Run vsim with `--resume-from-qemu state_in.bin --qemu-binary /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64`. Verify the QEMU side processes the ecall and returns 0.
191	
192	### Phase 4 - Kanata-loop integration
193	
194	**Goal**: existing `--kanata-loop-start <pc> --kanata-loop-end <pc>` flags drive hybrid mode automatically. **No magic CSR required in the binary.**
195	
196	New files:
197	- `hybrid/scripts/vsim-hybrid` (Python orchestrator):
198	  1. Inspect ELF + user's loop PCs.
199	  2. Launch QEMU with hybrid plugin in **PC-trigger mode** (`enter_pc=<start>`, `exit_pc=<end>` plugin args).
200	  3. On QEMU exit, launch vsim in resume mode with state file + the kanata flags unchanged.
201	  4. On vsim's exit-detail, optionally re-launch QEMU to finish.
202	
203	Files modified:
204	- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - PC-trigger mode added; cheap with `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()`.
205	- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - sibling `PcTrigger` class for `--hybrid-enter-pc` / `--hybrid-exit-pc` CLI flags.
206	- `src/main.cpp` - `--hybrid-mode=auto` aliases `--kanata-loop-start/end` to the hybrid PC triggers.
207	
208	Test: boot a benchmark from `external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/`, hand off at the steady-state inner loop, record kanata for N iterations in vsim, hand back to QEMU. The recorded kanata file for the captured iterations must be **byte-identical** to a fully-vsim baseline run for the same window (after `kanata_loop_skip` warmup).
209	
210	### Phase 5 - Hardening
211	
212	Each item independently scoped:
213	- **Vector regfile transfer**: measure DM cost; if >100ms per handoff, add mmap-shared V regfile via DPI (mirror the `set_ilm()` pattern in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:393-441`).
214	- **FP regs and `mstatus.FS`**: standard handling.
215	- **PMP**: all 64 pmpaddr/pmpcfg via Abstract Commands.
216	- **Priv-mode transitions**: set DCSR.prv so `dret` resumes in the right mode.
217	- **Traps inside ROI**: trap stays in vsim until exit-CSR retires.
218	- **MMIO touch in ROI**: add an interconnect watch (`src/platform/interconnect.hpp`), fail loudly on non-RAM addresses during the ROI window.
219	- **mcycle/minstret discontinuity**: sum across handoffs to keep monotonic; document the gap vs. fully-RTL.
220	- **lr/sc reservations**: explicitly invalidate via DCSR after resume.
221	- **Multi-hart**: ABI struct already carries per-hart arrays; `_NDS_NHART <= 2` already enforced (`src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:37-39`).
222	
223	Test: torture workload running interrupt-driven code, V+FP active, traps inside ROI, verifying mcycle/minstret monotonic across multiple handoffs.
224	
225	---
226	
227	## 6. Critical Files
228	
229	To modify:
230	- `src/platform/simple_memory.hpp` - mmap-backed storage (Phase 1)
231	- `src/platform/platform.hpp` - thread the mmap fd (Phase 1)
232	- `src/main.cpp` - new CLI flags (Phases 1-4)
233	- `src/simulator.hpp` - resume-from-qemu hook in `reset()`, csr_trigger tick in `run()` (Phases 2-3)
234	- `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - new build option (Phase 0)
235	
236	To reuse without modification:
237	- `src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp` - in-process DM driver
238	- `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp` - retire signal taps
239	- `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:91-100` - GPR/PC probes
240	- `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp` - LIEF-based ELF pre-scan for trigger PCs
241	- `src/main.cpp:183-214` - existing `--kanata-loop-*` flag parsing
242	
243	To create:
244	- `hybrid/include/state_abi.h`
245	- `hybrid/include/state_abi_check.h`
246	- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` + `meson.build`
247	- `hybrid/scripts/{test_dm_coverage.tcl, vsim-hybrid}`
248	- `src/hybrid/{qemu_resume.hpp, state_drain.hpp, csr_trigger.hpp}`
249	
250	External, reused as-is:
251	- `/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0)
252	- `/local/nick/qemu_v5/contrib/plugins/execlog.c` (template)
253	
254	---
255	
256	## 7. Verification
257	
258	End-to-end ladder, each level requires the previous:
259	
260	1. **Phase 0 build smoke**: `cmake -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON .. && make` succeeds; plugin builds; static_asserts pass.
261	2. **Phase 1 mmap parity**: `tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.cpp` passes - external mmap reader sees identical bytes to vsim's view.
262	3. **Phase 2 one-way correctness**: tiny program ends with `a2==3` and exit 0 after handoff.
263	4. **Phase 3 round-trip**: tiny program with `csrwi 0x7C0, 1; ecall` exits 0; intermediate state file is consumed and discarded.
264	5. **Phase 4 kanata equivalence**: for a benchmark in `external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/`, the kanata file produced by hybrid mode (with appropriate `kanata_loop_skip` warmup) is byte-identical to the fully-RTL baseline for the same loop window.
265	6. **Phase 5 torture**: traps + V + FP + multi-handoff, mcycle monotonic, no MMIO violations.
266	
267	Speed measurement at each phase: capture wall-clock for a representative workload (e.g., dhrystone, an existing testbench in `external/`). Target after Phase 4: **>=100x** speedup on workloads where the ROI is <1% of dynamic instructions.
268	
269	Each phase commit follows the project's TDD discipline (see `~/personal_git/.claude/TDD.md`): a failing test first, then the smallest code to pass, then refactor. Commits prefixed `structural:` or `behavioral:`.

273	## 8. Phase 3 E2E Test Expansion
274	
275	### 8.1 Context
276	
277	Phase 3 landed the round-trip mechanism. The existing e2e harness `tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh` exercises the full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 path but with a single fixture (`handoff_roundtrip.S`) that only validates ONE GPR (`a2 == 3`) via the semihosting exit code. The plumbing works, but two large coverage gaps remain before we move on to Phase 4:
278	
279	- **Register coverage**: 1 of 31 GPRs is observed; ABI-special regs (`sp`/`gp`/`tp`/`ra`/`fp`) are never touched; sign-bit and full-64-bit values are not exercised; the `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` trigger form (`csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1`) is never fired in e2e.
280	- **Memory coverage**: zero tests today. Vsim never executes a load/store between the entry and exit triggers, so the integration of the cache/memory subsystem with the resumed state is unverified. Cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap is also untested at e2e level (Phase 1 isolated unit test only).
281	
282	### 8.2 What's actually transferable today (constraints on fixture design)
283	
284	From `state_drain.hpp:28-45`, `resume_driver.hpp:46-68`, `qemu_handback.hpp:122-144`, `gdbstub_client.hpp:66-82`, and `hybrid_handoff.c:101-124`:
285	
286	| State class | Fully round-tripped? |
287	|---|---|
288	| `x1..x31`, `pc` | yes |
289	| `priv` | hardcoded to 3 in both directions |
290	| `f0..f31`, all CSRs (mstatus/mepc/mtvec/...), V regs, PMP | NOT transferred (Phase 5) |
291	| Memory written in vsim, read in QEMU 2 | NOT transferred unless `--shared-mem-path` is wired into `qemu_handback.hpp` (currently it isn't) |
292	
293	Implication: every Tier-1 fixture must encode pass/fail into a **GPR before the exit csrwi**, then in the QEMU 2 phase (after the csrwi, where DPC lands) verify and ship the result through `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. We cannot use FP/CSR/V state as test channels yet.
294	
295	### 8.3 Fixture three-phase template
296	
297	Every new fixture follows this layout (text base `0x80000000`, semihosting param block at `0x80001000` per the existing convention):
298	
299	```
300	_start:                          // PHASE A: runs in QEMU 1 (functional)
301	    <set up initial state>
302	    csrwi   0x7C0, 0             // QEMU drains x1..x31+pc, exits
303	
304	phase_b:                         // PHASE B: runs in vsim (RTL, cycle-accurate)
305	    <transform state under test>
306	    csrwi   0x7C0, 1             // vsim drains, exits 200
307	                                 //   on the way out, main spawns QEMU 2
308	                                 //   gdbstub restores x1..x31+pc, sends c
309	phase_c:                         // PHASE C: runs in QEMU 2 (functional)
310	    <fold result into checksum/bitmask in t2>
311	    li      t0, 0x80001000       // semihosting param block
312	    li      t1, 0x20026          // ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit
313	    sd      t1, 0(t0)
314	    sd      t2, 8(t0)            // exit_code = checksum (0 == pass)
315	    li      a0, 0x20             // SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED
316	    mv      a1, t0
317	    .option push; .option norvc
318	    slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7
319	    .option pop
320	1:  j 1b
321	```
322	
323	The single observable signal remains the QEMU 2 process exit code. Per fixture, the bash driver asserts that code matches an expected value (usually 0).
324	
325	### 8.4 Tier-1 test inventory (no infrastructure changes beyond fixtures)
326	
327	| Fixture | What it verifies | Pass criterion |
328	|---|---|---|
329	| `rt_all_gprs.S` | All 31 GPRs survive QEMU1->vsim->QEMU2. Phase A loads `x_i = 0xA000_0000_0000_0000 + i` for `i=1..31`. Phase B XORs every `x_i` with mask `K=0xDEADBEEFCAFEBABE`. Phase C checks each `x_i == (0xA000_0000_0000_0000+i) ^ K` and sets bit `i` in `t2` on mismatch. | exit 0 (no failed register) |
330	| `rt_abi_aliases.S` | Pass-through fidelity for `ra(x1)`, `sp(x2)`, `gp(x3)`, `tp(x4)`, `fp(x8)`. Phase A sets each to a distinct page-aligned constant in [0x8010_0000..0x8050_0000]. Phase B does NOT touch them (only csrwi entry+exit). Phase C verifies every alias unchanged; exit-code = first-mismatch index or 0. | exit 0 |
331	| `rt_sign_bits.S` | 64-bit width fidelity through DM Abstract Commands. Phase A loads `x10..x14` with `0`, `-1`, `0x8000_0000_0000_0000`, `0x7FFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFF`, `0x0000_0000_FFFF_FFFF`. Phase B is a single nop. Phase C compares element-wise. | exit 0 (no truncation/sign-ext bug) |
332	| `rt_pc_jump.S` | PC fidelity at the exit boundary. Phase B does `j .+0x40` so the exit csrwi sits 64 bytes ahead of where Phase B started; verifies DPC captured by `state_drain` reflects the post-jump PC, and QEMU 2 lands on Phase C at the right offset. Phase C just exits 0. Wrong PC -> illegal insn / hang -> bash timeout fails the test. | exit 0 |
333	| `rt_runtime_kind.S` | The `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` trigger path (`csrrw zero,0x7C0,x10` where `x10=1`). Phase A: `li x10, 1`, then `csrrw zero, 0x7C0, x10` (exit-via-runtime form). Wait - this fires at QEMU first, not vsim. Better split: Phase A uses `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to enter; Phase B has `li x10,1; csrrw x0,0x7C0,x10` to exit through the runtime decode in `handoff_controller.hpp:42-44`. Verifies `decode_csrrw_rs1` + GPR-read-by-rs1 path. | exit 0 |
334	| `rt_long_roi.S` | Vsim runs a non-trivial ROI: 100-iteration counted loop summing `1..100` into `x10` (expected 5050). Phase B is the loop; Phase C verifies `x10 == 5050`. Catches resume-state corruption only visible after many cycles, and exercises branch/decode through the cycle-accurate pipeline. | exit 0 |
335	| `rt_mem_loadstore.S` | Vsim memory subsystem under load while resumed from foreign state. Phase A initializes a 64-byte buffer at `0x8000_2000` to a known pattern via `sd` from x10. Phase B reads the buffer back word-by-word, XORs with a constant, writes back, then re-reads and folds into x10 (final checksum). Phase C compares x10 to the pre-computed expected. Exercises load-after-store, RAW within vsim. | exit 0 |
336	| `rt_unaligned_lh.S` | Halfword/byte loads at unaligned addresses inside vsim ROI. Phase A stores 0xCAFEBABEDEADBEEF at 0x8000_3000 and 0xCAFEBABEDEADBEEF at 0x8000_3008. Phase B does `lh x10, 1(t0)`, `lb x11, 3(t0)`, `lhu x12, 5(t0)`, accumulates into x10. Phase C compares against expected. | exit 0 |

338	### 8.5 Tier-2 test (requires Phase 1 wiring into handback)
339	
340	**`rt_shared_mem.sh`**: cross-simulator memory survival via mmap. This is the only test that detects regressions in "memory written in vsim is visible to QEMU 2" - the key promise of the hybrid architecture beyond Phase 3.
341	
342	Required code change (small, but a real change beyond test scaffolding):
343	- `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:84-116` (`detail::spawn_qemu`) accepts an optional `shared_mem_path`. When set, `argv` is rebuilt to add `-object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=<path>,size=<size>` and `-machine virt,memory-backend=mem` (replacing `-M virt`).
344	- `src/main.cpp` plumbs the existing `--shared-mem-path` / `--shared-mem-size` flags into `run_qemu_handback`.
345	- Bash driver creates `/dev/shm/vsim-rt-shared` of the right size, launches QEMU 1 with `-object memory-backend-file,...,share=on`, vsim with `--shared-mem-path`, and asserts.
346	
347	Fixture (`rt_shared_mem.S`):
348	- Phase A: write magic byte pattern (e.g., `0xA5` x 4096) to `0x8001_0000` from x10, csrwi entry.
349	- Phase B: vsim verifies pattern is intact (loop reading 4 KB into checksum), XORs page in place with `0x5A`, stores result of mask check to x10, csrwi exit.
350	- Phase C: QEMU 2 reads the same 4 KB through the shared mmap, recomputes checksum (should match `0xA5 ^ 0x5A` = `0xFF` everywhere), folds mismatch count into exit code.
351	
352	Pass criterion: exit 0 AND wall-clock confirms two-sided visibility (page must be modified by both simulators).
353	
354	> **NOTE**: Tier-2 is currently **blocked** by the memory-layout mismatch described in Section 9. The fixture, driver, Makefile entry, and CMake entry are already in tree; the actual e2e will only PASS after Section 9's redesign lands.
